=== 九州地方 === *[[福岡県]][[久留米市]][[御井町]][[高良山]]にある[[祇園山古墳]]は3世紀中期のものとされる一辺が約23メートルの方墳であり{{Sfn|石山|大川|高橋|1979}}、規模や様式などが[[魏志倭人伝]]の記述に類似するとして、[[卑弥呼]]の墓に比定する説がある{{Sfn|宝賀|2001|pp=62-95}}。 *[[大分県]][[大分市]]の[[古宮古墳]]は、九州で唯一の[[横口式石槨]]をもつ古墳である。 *[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の[[常心塚古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ3.8メートルを測り、一辺40メートルの土塁を巡らす。 全体的な構造は[[石舞台古墳]]に類似する。
== 脚注 == <references />
==参考文献== *{{Cite book|和書|year=1979-03-24|date=1979-03-24|author=石山勲|author2=大川清|author3=高橋章|chapter=祇園山古墳の調査|title=福岡県久留米市所在祇園山・七曲山両古墳群の調査（九州縦貫自動車道関係埋蔵文化財調査報告27）|publisher=福岡県教育委員会|ncid=BN04478590|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite journal|和書|year=2001-12-20|date=2001-12-20|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|title=卑弥呼の冢補論-祇園山古墳とその周辺-|url=http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/himikotyou/himiko3horon.htm|journal=季刊・古代史の海|volume=26|pages=62-96|publisher=「古代史の海」の会|issn=13415522|naid=40005104602|ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]] *[[円墳]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ほうふん}} [[Category:日本の考古学]] [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:方墳|*]]

'''方墳'''（ほうふん）は、墳丘の平面形が方形になる[[古墳]]のことで、古墳時代の全期間にわたって[[円墳]]についで数多く築かれている。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]初頭ないし前期に築かれるものは、[[弥生時代]]の大和風の[[方形周溝墓]]や出雲風の[[四隅突出型墳丘墓]]の延長と考えられ、性格自体もほとんど変わらないとみなされているが、最近は、[[前方後円墳]]や[[土師器]]出現の時期を画期として、「方墳」の一種とみなされている。そのため「前期群集墳」という造語まで発生している。弥生時代の[[四隅突出型墳丘墓]]は、すでに古墳時代に作られる技術が多様に用いられていることから、古代出雲の技術を物語る。<ref>『知識ゼロからの古墳入門』72-73頁 広瀬 和雄 （監修） </ref>
前期方墳の代表例は、島根県安来市の大成古墳、[[造山古墳 (安来市)|造山古墳]]（1辺60m）が全国最大の規模を誇る。方墳は出雲に集中しており、前方後方墳の魁もみられる。比較的小型のものが多いが、前方後円墳の築造が停止する7世紀には有力者の古墳の墳形に採用されている。
例としては現在、[[用明天皇]]陵に指定されている[[大阪]]春日向山古墳（辺長63m×60m）や、[[推古天皇]]陵に指定されている山田高塚古墳（辺長63m×56m）、[[蘇我馬子]]の墓と考えられている[[石舞台古墳]]（[[上円下方墳]]との説あり）、[[千葉県]]の[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]（辺長80m、高さ13m）などがある。6世紀末に築造された巨大方墳、[[赤坂天王山古墳]]（桜井市倉橋、東西45.5メートル、南北42.2メートル、高さ約9.1メートル）を、崇峻陵とする[[森浩一]]の説が有力視されている。
大きさでは、奈良県の[[桝山古墳]]が全国1位で、千葉県の龍角寺岩屋古墳（一辺約78メートル）が第2位である。
== 各地の方墳 == === 東北地方 === *[[飯野坂古墳群]]（いいのざか、宮城県名取市飯野坂山居、方墳二基（観音堂北一・二号墳）、このほかに山囲古墳（後期）や薬師丸堂西北副古墳（別称:唐戸塚古墳、現在消滅、4世紀末から5世紀の築造）の二方墳があった。）
=== 関東地方 === *[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]]にある[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]は、一辺78メートルの[[終末期古墳|終末期]]最大の方墳であり同時期に造営された[[用明天皇]]陵（[[春日向山古墳]]）や [[推古天皇]]陵（[[山田高塚古墳]]）を凌ぐものである。 *千葉県[[山武市]]に所在する板附古墳群中の[[駄ノ塚古墳]]は、一辺60メートルの終末期の大方墳であり、[[発掘調査]]の結果[[610年]]（推古天皇18年）から[[620年]]（同28年）の間に造営された可能性が高いことが判明した。 *[[宝塔山古墳]]は、群馬県[[前橋市]][[総社町 (群馬県)|総社町]]総社に所在する古墳時代終末期の大型方墳である。各辺は東辺54.5メートル、西辺51.2メートル、南辺49メートル、北辺42メートルで、高さ12メートルである。 *[[蛇穴山古墳]]は、群馬県前橋市総社町に所在し、一辺39メートル前後と考えられており、古墳時代再終末期に属する。
=== 中部・東海地方 === *[[静岡市]]宮ヶ崎町の賤機山（しずはたやま）古墳は、一辺23メートル前後、高さ5メートル余の方墳と考えられている。1953年（昭和28年）3月31日国の史跡に指定された。 *[[木曽川]]と[[飛騨川]]の合流する左岸段丘上に河合古墳群がある。中でも次郎兵衛塚1号墳は一辺が約30メートルの方墳で、周壕を持ち、墳丘全体が河原石で覆われている。[[葺石]]か。石室を三つ持ち、大量の[[須恵器]]・玉類・耳環などの副葬品が検出された。県内では15基確認されており、[[7世紀]]に入って築造されたものと考えられる。<ref>谷口和人「日本の真ん中岐阜のあけぼの」 松田之利・谷口和人・筧敏生・所文隆・上村恵宏・黒田隆志『岐阜県の歴史』山川出版社 2000年 31-32ページ</ref>
=== 北陸地方 === *[[石川県]]鹿島郡能登町字須曽に所在する[[須曽蝦夷穴古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ約4.5メートルの古墳時代後期に属する方墳である。
=== 近畿地方 === *[[1999年]]（平成11年）に奈良県[[御所市]]柳田町の鴨都波遺跡で発見された1号墳は、南北辺20メートル、東西辺16メートルの小方墳であるが、[[粘土槨]]のうちから三面の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が検出された。今までは同鏡を出土する古墳は各地の首長墓からであり、前方後円墳であった。築造のころには三角縁神獣鏡が多数流通していたと考えられる。 *[[橿原市]]四条町の4条1号墳は、5世紀後半の方形墳で木製埴輪が出土している。天武朝初期に削平され、道路や側溝（のちの[[藤原京]]の条坊に一致する）が敷設された。 *[[御所市]]大字室に所在する[[室宮山古墳#陪塚|ネコ塚古墳]]は、一辺63メートルの大型方墳である。葛城地方最大の前方後円墳[[室宮山古墳]]の北東の周濠に接した陪塚として築造されている。 *奈良県[[桜井市]]倉橋に所在する[[赤坂天王山古墳]]は、東西約42.2メートル、高さ9.1メートルの大型の方墳である。6世紀末から7世紀初めの築造と考えられており、明治年間まで「崇峻天皇倉梯岡上陵」として取り扱われていた。 *[[平野塚穴山古墳]]は奈良県[[香芝市]]平野字土山に所在する一辺18メートル、高さ4メートルの終末期の方墳。[[高市皇子]]・[[茅渟王]]・当麻氏などの被葬者論がある。1973年（昭和48年）国の史跡に指定されている。7世紀後半でも古い時期と考えられている。 *大阪府[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]の葉室塚古墳（一辺62メートル）、前方後円墳終焉後（[[終末期古墳]]）。 *[[和歌山市]]の[[岩橋千塚古墳群|岩橋千塚]]の井辺1号墳・2号墳や[[御坊市]]の岩内1号墳などが方墳であり、このうちの井辺1号墳は一辺40メートルもあり、岩橋千塚最後の首長墓に相応しい規模である。
=== 中国地方 === *[[島根県]]東部の[[出雲]]地方は方墳が特に多い地域として知られている。景初三年名をもつ[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した[[神原神社|神原神社古墳]]は一辺が30メートルの前期前半の方墳である。6世紀後半になると[[山代方墳]]、その後永久宅後古墳がつくられている。浜田市の片山古墳、益田市の鵜ノ鼻古墳群50号墳、赤来町の長者原古墳などの方墳が存在する。7世紀の半ばには松江市の廻原1号墳は、埋葬施設が刳り抜きの石棺状施設に横口を開けた珍しい構造で、旧領斜面を加工してつくられた小さいながらも見事な方墳である<ref>渡辺貞幸「地域王権の時代」 松尾寿・田中義昭・渡辺貞幸・大日方克己・井上寛司・竹永三男『島根県の歴史』2005年 77-78ページ</ref>。
=== 四国地方 === *
=== 九州地方 === *[[福岡県]][[久留米市]][[御井町]][[高良山]]にある[[祇園山古墳]]は3世紀中期のものとされる一辺が約23メートルの方墳であり{{Sfn|石山|大川|高橋|1979}}、規模や様式などが[[魏志倭人伝]]の記述に類似するとして、[[卑弥呼]]の墓に比定する説がある{{Sfn|宝賀|2001|pp=62-95}}。 *[[大分県]][[大分市]]の[[古宮古墳]]は、九州で唯一の[[横口式石槨]]をもつ古墳である。 *[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の[[常心塚古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ3.8メートルを測り、一辺40メートルの土塁を巡らす。 全体的な構造は[[石舞台古墳]]に類似する。
== 脚注 == <references />
==参考文献== *{{Cite book|和書|year=1979-03-24|date=1979-03-24|author=石山勲|author2=大川清|author3=高橋章|chapter=祇園山古墳の調査|title=福岡県久留米市所在祇園山・七曲山両古墳群の調査（九州縦貫自動車道関係埋蔵文化財調査報告27）|publisher=福岡県教育委員会|ncid=BN04478590|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite journal|和書|year=2001-12-20|date=2001-12-20|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|title=卑弥呼の冢補論-祇園山古墳とその周辺-|url=http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/himikotyou/himiko3horon.htm|journal=季刊・古代史の海|volume=26|pages=62-96|publisher=「古代史の海」の会|issn=13415522|naid=40005104602|ref=harv}}

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|方墳の例・[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]（千葉県印旛郡栄町）]]
'''方墳'''（ほうふん）は、墳丘の平面形が方形になる[[古墳]]のことで、[[古墳時代]]の全期間にわたって[[円墳]]についで数多く築かれている。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]初頭ないし前期に築かれるものは、[[弥生時代]]の[[畿内]]風の[[方形周溝墓]]や[[山陰]]風の[[四隅突出型墳丘墓]]の延長と考えられ、性格自体もほとんど変わらないとみなされているが、最近は、[[前方後円墳]]や[[土師器]]出現の時期を画期として、「方墳」の一種とみなされている。そのため「前期群集墳」という造語まで発生している。弥生時代の[[四隅突出型墳丘墓]]は、すでに古墳時代に作られる技術が多様に用いられていることから、古代出雲の技術を物語る。<ref>『知識ゼロからの古墳入門』72-73頁 広瀬 和雄 （監修） </ref>
前期方墳の代表例は、島根県安来市の大成古墳、[[造山古墳 (安来市)|造山古墳]]（1辺60m）が全国最大の規模を誇る。方墳は出雲に集中しており、前方後方墳の魁もみられる。比較的小型のものが多いが、前方後円墳の築造が停止する7世紀には有力者の古墳の墳形に採用されている。
例としては現在、[[用明天皇]]陵に指定されている[[大阪]]春日向山古墳（辺長63m×60m）や、[[推古天皇]]陵に指定されている山田高塚古墳（辺長63m×56m）、[[蘇我馬子]]の墓と考えられている[[石舞台古墳]]（[[上円下方墳]]との説あり）、[[千葉県]]の[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]（辺長80m、高さ13m）などがある。6世紀末に築造された巨大方墳、[[赤坂天王山古墳]]（桜井市倉橋、東西45.5メートル、南北42.2メートル、高さ約9.1メートル）を、崇峻陵とする[[森浩一]]の説が有力視されている。
大きさでは、奈良県の[[桝山古墳]]が全国1位で、千葉県の龍角寺岩屋古墳（一辺約78メートル）が第2位である。
== 各地の方墳 == === 東北地方 === *[[飯野坂古墳群]]（いいのざか、宮城県名取市飯野坂山居、方墳二基（観音堂北一・二号墳）、このほかに山囲古墳（後期）や薬師丸堂西北副古墳（別称:唐戸塚古墳、現在消滅、4世紀末から5世紀の築造）の二方墳があった。）
=== 関東地方 === *[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]]にある[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]は、一辺78メートルの[[終末期古墳|終末期]]最大の方墳であり同時期に造営された[[用明天皇]]陵（[[春日向山古墳]]）や [[推古天皇]]陵（[[山田高塚古墳]]）を凌ぐものである。 *千葉県[[山武市]]に所在する板附古墳群中の[[駄ノ塚古墳]]は、一辺60メートルの終末期の大方墳であり、[[発掘調査]]の結果[[610年]]（推古天皇18年）から[[620年]]（同28年）の間に造営された可能性が高いことが判明した。 *[[宝塔山古墳]]は、群馬県[[前橋市]][[総社町 (群馬県)|総社町]]総社に所在する古墳時代終末期の大型方墳である。各辺は東辺54.5メートル、西辺51.2メートル、南辺49メートル、北辺42メートルで、高さ12メートルである。 *[[蛇穴山古墳]]は、群馬県前橋市総社町に所在し、一辺39メートル前後と考えられており、古墳時代再終末期に属する。

=== 北陸地方 === *[[石川県]]鹿島郡能登町字須曽に所在する[[須曽蝦夷穴古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ約4.5メートルの古墳時代後期に属する方墳である。

=== 中国地方 === *[[島根県]]東部の[[出雲]]地方は方墳が特に多い地域として知られている。景初三年名をもつ[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した[[神原神社|神原神社古墳]]は一辺が30メートルの前期前半の方墳である。6世紀後半になると[[山代方墳]]、その後永久宅後古墳がつくられている。浜田市の片山古墳、益田市の鵜ノ鼻古墳群50号墳、赤来町の長者原古墳などの方墳が存在する。7世紀の半ばには松江市の廻原1号墳は、埋葬施設が刳り抜きの石棺状施設に横口を開けた珍しい構造で、旧領斜面を加工してつくられた小さいながらも見事な方墳である<ref>渡辺貞幸「地域王権の時代」 松尾寿・田中義昭・渡辺貞幸・大日方克己・井上寛司・竹永三男『島根県の歴史』2005年 77-78ページ</ref>。
=== 四国地方 === *
=== 九州地方 === *[[福岡県]][[久留米市]][[御井町]][[高良山]]にある[[祇園山古墳]]は3世紀中期のものとされる一辺が約23メートルの方墳であり{{Sfn|石山|大川|高橋|1979}}、規模や様式などが[[魏志倭人伝]]の記述に類似するとして、[[卑弥呼]]の墓に比定する説がある{{Sfn|宝賀|2001|pp=62-95}}。 *[[大分県]][[大分市]]の[[古宮古墳]]は、九州で唯一の[[横口式石槨]]をもつ古墳である。 *[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の[[常心塚古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ3.8メートルを測り、一辺40メートルの土塁を巡らす。 全体的な構造は[[石舞台古墳]]に類似する。
== 脚注 == <references />
==参考文献== *{{Cite book|和書|year=1979-03-24|date=1979-03-24|author=石山勲|author2=大川清|author3=高橋章|chapter=祇園山古墳の調査|title=福岡県久留米市所在祇園山・七曲山両古墳群の調査（九州縦貫自動車道関係埋蔵文化財調査報告27）|publisher=福岡県教育委員会|ncid=BN04478590|ref=harv}}

== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[古墳]] *[[円墳]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ほうふん}} [[Category:日本の考古学]] [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:方墳|*]]

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|方墳の例・[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]（千葉県印旛郡栄町）]]
'''方墳'''（ほうふん）は、墳丘の平面形が方形になる[[古墳]]のことで、[[古墳時代]]の全期間にわたって[[円墳]]についで数多く築かれている。
== 概要 == [[古墳時代]]初頭ないし前期に築かれるものは、[[弥生時代]]の[[畿内]]風の[[方形周溝墓]]や[[山陰]]風の[[四隅突出型墳丘墓]]の延長と考えられ、性格自体もほとんど変わらないとみなされているが、最近は、[[前方後円墳]]や[[土師器]]出現の時期を画期として、「方墳」の一種とみなされている。そのため「前期群集墳」という造語まで発生している。弥生時代の[[四隅突出型墳丘墓]]は、すでに古墳時代に作られる技術が多様に用いられていることから、古代出雲の技術を物語る。<ref>『知識ゼロからの古墳入門』72-73頁 広瀬 和雄 （監修） </ref>
前期方墳の代表例は、島根県安来市の大成古墳、[[造山古墳 (安来市)|造山古墳]]（1辺60m）が全国最大の規模を誇る。方墳は出雲に集中しており、前方後方墳の魁もみられる。比較的小型のものが多いが、前方後円墳の築造が停止する7世紀には有力者の古墳の墳形に採用されている。
例としては現在、[[用明天皇]]陵に指定されている[[大阪]]春日向山古墳（辺長63m×60m）や、[[推古天皇]]陵に指定されている山田高塚古墳（辺長63m×56m）、[[蘇我馬子]]の墓と考えられている[[石舞台古墳]]（[[上円下方墳]]との説あり）、[[千葉県]]の[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]（辺長80m、高さ13m）などがある。6世紀末に築造された巨大方墳、[[赤坂天王山古墳]]（桜井市倉橋、東西45.5メートル、南北42.2メートル、高さ約9.1メートル）を、崇峻陵とする[[森浩一]]の説が有力視されている。
大きさでは、奈良県の[[桝山古墳]]が全国1位で、千葉県の龍角寺岩屋古墳（一辺約78メートル）が第2位である。
== 各地の方墳 == === 東北地方 === *[[飯野坂古墳群]]（いいのざか、宮城県名取市飯野坂山居、方墳二基（観音堂北一・二号墳）、このほかに山囲古墳（後期）や薬師丸堂西北副古墳（別称:唐戸塚古墳、現在消滅、4世紀末から5世紀の築造）の二方墳があった。）
=== 関東地方 === *[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[栄町]]にある[[龍角寺岩屋古墳]]は、一辺78メートルの[[終末期古墳|終末期]]最大の方墳であり同時期に造営された[[用明天皇]]陵（[[春日向山古墳]]）や [[推古天皇]]陵（[[山田高塚古墳]]）を凌ぐものである。 *千葉県[[山武市]]に所在する板附古墳群中の[[駄ノ塚古墳]]は、一辺60メートルの終末期の大方墳であり、[[発掘調査]]の結果[[610年]]（推古天皇18年）から[[620年]]（同28年）の間に造営された可能性が高いことが判明した。 *[[宝塔山古墳]]は、群馬県[[前橋市]][[総社町 (群馬県)|総社町]]総社に所在する古墳時代終末期の大型方墳である。各辺は東辺54.5メートル、西辺51.2メートル、南辺49メートル、北辺42メートルで、高さ12メートルである。 *[[蛇穴山古墳]]は、群馬県前橋市総社町に所在し、一辺39メートル前後と考えられており、古墳時代再終末期に属する。
=== 中部・東海地方 === *[[木曽川]]と[[飛騨川]]の合流する左岸段丘上に河合古墳群がある。中でも次郎兵衛塚1号墳は一辺が約30メートルの方墳で、周溝を持ち、墳丘全体が河原石で覆われている。[[葺石]]か。石室を3つ持ち、大量の[[須恵器]]・玉類・[[耳環]]などの副葬品が検出された。県内では15基確認されており、[[7世紀]]に入って築造されたものと考えられる<ref>谷口和人「日本の真ん中岐阜のあけぼの」松田之利・谷口和人・筧敏生・所文隆・上村恵宏・黒田隆志『岐阜県の歴史』山川出版社 2000年 31-32ページ</ref>。
=== 北陸地方 === *[[石川県]]鹿島郡能登町字須曽に所在する[[須曽蝦夷穴古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ約4.5メートルの古墳時代後期に属する方墳である。
=== 近畿地方 === *[[1999年]]（平成11年）に奈良県[[御所市]]柳田町の鴨都波遺跡で発見された1号墳は、南北辺20メートル、東西辺16メートルの小方墳であるが、[[粘土槨]]のうちから3面の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が検出された。今までは同鏡を出土する古墳は各地の首長墓からであり、前方後円墳であった。築造のころには三角縁神獣鏡が多数流通していたと考えられる。 *[[橿原市]]四条町の4条1号墳は、5世紀後半の方形墳で木製埴輪が出土している。天武朝初期に削平され、道路や側溝（のちの[[藤原京]]の条坊に一致する）が敷設された。 *[[御所市]]大字室に所在する[[室宮山古墳#陪塚|ネコ塚古墳]]は、一辺63メートルの大型方墳である。葛城地方最大の前方後円墳[[室宮山古墳]]の北東の周濠に接した陪塚として築造されている。 *奈良県[[桜井市]]倉橋に所在する[[赤坂天王山古墳]]は、東西約42.2メートル、高さ9.1メートルの大型の方墳である。6世紀末から7世紀初めの築造と考えられており、明治年間まで「崇峻天皇倉梯岡上陵」として取り扱われていた。 *[[平野塚穴山古墳]]は奈良県[[香芝市]]平野字土山に所在する一辺18メートル、高さ4メートルの終末期の方墳。[[高市皇子]]・[[茅渟王]]・当麻氏などの被葬者論がある。1973年（昭和48年）国の史跡に指定されている。7世紀後半でも古い時期と考えられている。 *大阪府[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]の葉室塚古墳（一辺62メートル）、前方後円墳終焉後（[[終末期古墳]]）。 *[[和歌山市]]の[[岩橋千塚古墳群|岩橋千塚]]の井辺1号墳・2号墳や[[御坊市]]の岩内1号墳などが方墳であり、このうちの井辺1号墳は一辺40メートルもあり、岩橋千塚最後の首長墓に相応しい規模である。
=== 中国地方 === *[[島根県]]東部の[[出雲]]地方は方墳が特に多い地域として知られている。景初三年名をもつ[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した[[神原神社|神原神社古墳]]は一辺が30メートルの前期前半の方墳である。6世紀後半になると[[山代方墳]]、その後永久宅後古墳がつくられている。浜田市の片山古墳、益田市の鵜ノ鼻古墳群50号墳、赤来町の長者原古墳などの方墳が存在する。7世紀の半ばには松江市の廻原1号墳は、埋葬施設が刳り抜きの石棺状施設に横口を開けた珍しい構造で、旧領斜面を加工してつくられた小さいながらも見事な方墳である<ref>渡辺貞幸「地域王権の時代」 松尾寿・田中義昭・渡辺貞幸・大日方克己・井上寛司・竹永三男『島根県の歴史』2005年 77-78ページ</ref>。
=== 四国地方 === *高知県香美市土佐山田町楠目 伏原大塚古墳
四国最大の方墳で、発掘物から女性の装飾品が多数出土したので 高貴な女性を埋葬したと考えられている。
土佐邪馬壹国説では この古墳を卑弥呼の御陵と比定している。
=== 九州地方 === *[[福岡県]][[久留米市]][[御井町]][[高良山]]にある[[祇園山古墳]]は3世紀中期のものとされる一辺が約23メートルの方墳であり{{Sfn|石山|大川|高橋|1979}}、規模や様式などが[[魏志倭人伝]]の記述に類似するとして、[[卑弥呼]]の墓に比定する説がある{{Sfn|宝賀|2001|pp=62-95}}。 *[[大分県]][[大分市]]の[[古宮古墳]]は、九州で唯一の[[横口式石槨]]をもつ古墳である。 *[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の[[常心塚古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ3.8メートルを測り、一辺40メートルの土塁を巡らす。 全体的な構造は[[石舞台古墳]]に類似する。

=== 中部・東海地方 === *[[木曽川]]と[[飛騨川]]の合流する左岸段丘上に河合古墳群がある。中でも次郎兵衛塚1号墳は一辺が約30メートルの方墳で、周溝を持ち、墳丘全体が河原石で覆われている。[[葺石]]か。石室を3つ持ち、大量の[[須恵器]]・玉類・[[耳環]]などの副葬品が検出された。県内では15基確認されており、[[7世紀]]に入って築造されたものと考えられる<ref>谷口和人「日本の真ん中岐阜のあけぼの」松田之利・谷口和人・筧敏生・所文隆・上村恵宏・黒田隆志『岐阜県の歴史』山川出版社 2000年 31-32ページ</ref>。
=== 北陸地方 === *[[石川県]]鹿島郡能登町字須曽に所在する[[須曽蝦夷穴古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ約4.5メートルの古墳時代後期に属する方墳である。
=== 近畿地方 === *[[1999年]]（平成11年）に奈良県[[御所市]]柳田町の鴨都波遺跡で発見された1号墳は、南北辺20メートル、東西辺16メートルの小方墳であるが、[[粘土槨]]のうちから3面の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が検出された。今までは同鏡を出土する古墳は各地の首長墓からであり、前方後円墳であった。築造のころには三角縁神獣鏡が多数流通していたと考えられる。 *[[橿原市]]四条町の4条1号墳は、5世紀後半の方形墳で木製埴輪が出土している。天武朝初期に削平され、道路や側溝（のちの[[藤原京]]の条坊に一致する）が敷設された。 *[[御所市]]大字室に所在する[[室宮山古墳#陪塚|ネコ塚古墳]]は、一辺63メートルの大型方墳である。葛城地方最大の前方後円墳[[室宮山古墳]]の北東の周濠に接した陪塚として築造されている。 *奈良県[[桜井市]]倉橋に所在する[[赤坂天王山古墳]]は、東西約42.2メートル、高さ9.1メートルの大型の方墳である。6世紀末から7世紀初めの築造と考えられており、明治年間まで「崇峻天皇倉梯岡上陵」として取り扱われていた。 *[[平野塚穴山古墳]]は奈良県[[香芝市]]平野字土山に所在する一辺18メートル、高さ4メートルの終末期の方墳。[[高市皇子]]・[[茅渟王]]・当麻氏などの被葬者論がある。1973年（昭和48年）国の史跡に指定されている。7世紀後半でも古い時期と考えられている。 *大阪府[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]の葉室塚古墳（一辺62メートル）、前方後円墳終焉後（[[終末期古墳]]）。 *[[和歌山市]]の[[岩橋千塚古墳群|岩橋千塚]]の井辺1号墳・2号墳や[[御坊市]]の岩内1号墳などが方墳であり、このうちの井辺1号墳は一辺40メートルもあり、岩橋千塚最後の首長墓に相応しい規模である。
=== 中国地方 === *[[島根県]]東部の[[出雲]]地方は方墳が特に多い地域として知られている。景初三年名をもつ[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が出土した[[神原神社|神原神社古墳]]は一辺が30メートルの前期前半の方墳である。6世紀後半になると[[山代方墳]]、その後永久宅後古墳がつくられている。浜田市の片山古墳、益田市の鵜ノ鼻古墳群50号墳、赤来町の長者原古墳などの方墳が存在する。7世紀の半ばには松江市の廻原1号墳は、埋葬施設が刳り抜きの石棺状施設に横口を開けた珍しい構造で、旧領斜面を加工してつくられた小さいながらも見事な方墳である<ref>渡辺貞幸「地域王権の時代」 松尾寿・田中義昭・渡辺貞幸・大日方克己・井上寛司・竹永三男『島根県の歴史』2005年 77-78ページ</ref>。
=== 四国地方 === *高知県香美市土佐山田町楠目 伏原大塚古墳
四国最大の方墳で、発掘物から女性の装飾品が多数出土したので 高貴な女性を埋葬したと考えられている。
土佐邪馬壹国説では この古墳を卑弥呼の御陵と比定している。
=== 九州地方 === *[[福岡県]][[久留米市]][[御井町]][[高良山]]にある[[祇園山古墳]]は3世紀中期のものとされる一辺が約23メートルの方墳であり{{Sfn|石山|大川|高橋|1979}}、規模や様式などが[[魏志倭人伝]]の記述に類似するとして、[[卑弥呼]]の墓に比定する説がある{{Sfn|宝賀|2001|pp=62-95}}。 *[[大分県]][[大分市]]の[[古宮古墳 (大分市)|古宮古墳]]は、九州で唯一の[[横口式石槨]]をもつ古墳である。 *[[宮崎県]][[西都市]]の[[常心塚古墳]]は、一辺約25メートル、高さ3.8メートルを測り、一辺40メートルの土塁を巡らす。 全体的な構造は[[石舞台古墳]]に類似する。
== 脚注 == <references />
==参考文献== *{{Cite book|和書|year=1979-03-24|date=1979-03-24|author=石山勲|author2=大川清|author3=高橋章|chapter=祇園山古墳の調査|title=福岡県久留米市所在祇園山・七曲山両古墳群の調査（九州縦貫自動車道関係埋蔵文化財調査報告27）|publisher=福岡県教育委員会|ncid=BN04478590|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite journal|和書|year=2001-12-20|date=2001-12-20|author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男|title=卑弥呼の冢補論-祇園山古墳とその周辺-|url=http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/himikotyou/himiko3horon.htm|journal=季刊・古代史の海|volume=26|pages=62-96|publisher=「古代史の海」の会|issn=13415522|naid=40005104602|ref=harv}}

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]] '''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to [[Marufun|circular mounds]]. == Overview == The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an ex..."
revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to [[Marufun|circular mounds]].
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} * {{ill|Marufun|ja|円墳}}

It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.

=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.

=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />

=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Square Kofun}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Square Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:方墳]]

It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. === Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Square Kofun}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Square Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Square tumuli]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:方墳]]

The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. === Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Square tumuli]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Square Kofun}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Square tumuli]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Square Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Square tumuli]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:方墳]]

The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.

The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. '''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />

'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]]

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]

The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.

=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.

=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.

revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square burial mound, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are burial mounds with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group burial mounds" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner burial mounds already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.

=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
