Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|12=draft}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|12=draft}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=draft}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=draft}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=draft}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=draft}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=draft}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|12=draft}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=draft}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=draft}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=draft}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=draft}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />

[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Square Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Square Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese archaeology]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Square Kofun]] [[ja:方墳]] [[sn:quare Kofun]] [[en:Square Kofun]] {{citation needed|Square Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Square Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}}

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=draft}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=draft}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.

The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]

A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft|12=draft}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft|12=draft}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibutai Kofun|qq=}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Umako|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|qq=}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himiko|qq=}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後方墳|" to "|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|"
revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibutai Kofun|qq=}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Umako|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|qq=}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himiko|qq=}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]

Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibutai Kofun|qq=}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Umako|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|qq=}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|ja|飯野坂古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himiko|qq=}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}.

The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} also present. Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|ja|石舞台古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibutai Kofun|qq=}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Umako|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The {{ill|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|ja|赤坂天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|qq=}} in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.

[[sn:Square Kofun]] [[ja:方墳]] [[sn:quare Kofun]] [[en:Square Kofun]]
<!--Square Kofun--><!--Square Kofun-->

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}

revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|方墳|version=99964515|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Kofun]]
[[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Square Kofun]]}}
[[sn:Square Kofun]] [[ja:方墳]] [[sn:quare Kofun]] [[en:Square Kofun]]

<!--Square Kofun--><!--Square Kofun--> [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:方墳]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:方墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|方墳|version=99964515|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Square Kofun]]}}

=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

<!--Square Kofun--><!--Square Kofun--> [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:方墳]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:方墳]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Tennōzan Kofun (Sakurai)|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennozan Kofun|Akasaka Tennozan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
