revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

[[en:Square Kofun]]
<!--Square Kofun--><!--Square Kofun--> [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:方墳]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:方墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11504353}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.

'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />

[[en:Square Kofun]]
<!--Square Kofun--><!--Square Kofun--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:方墳]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:方墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11504353}} [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]]'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The [[:en:Ishibutai Kofun|Ishibutai Kofun]], thought to be the tomb of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *[[:en:Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster]] (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11504353]] Wikidata name: square kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}}, and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|en|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11666712|lt=Iinozaka Kofun Cluster}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|en|Himiko|WD=Q234451|lt=Himiko}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}}, and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|en|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11666712|lt=Iinozaka Kofun Cluster}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|en|Himiko|WD=Q234451|lt=Himiko}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Square Kofun]][[ja:方墳]][[zh:方墳]]
[[File:Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An example of a square Kofun, {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Sakae, Inba District, Chiba Prefecture)]]
'''Hōfun''' (方墳) are Kofuns with a square-shaped tumulus. They were constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], second in number only to {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview ==
The square mounds built in the early to mid-[[Kofun period]] are considered an extension of the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}'s {{ill|Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki-style square-ditch tombs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} and the {{ill|San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}}. It is believed that their function and form underwent little change during this time. Recently, however, with the emergence of {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, square mounds are increasingly regarded as a distinct type, leading to terms such as "early group Kofuns" (前期群集墳). The San'in-style projecting-corner Kofuns already incorporated diverse techniques found in the Kofun period, showcasing the advanced technology of ancient Izumo.<ref>''Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,'' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), pp. 72–73.</ref>
A prominent early example is the {{ill|Ōnari Kofun|ja|造山古墳 (安来市)|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|lt=Ōnari Kofun|qq=ama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnari Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuriyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造山古墳群}} in Yasugi, Shimane Prefecture, which measures 60 meters on each side, making it the largest square mound in Japan. Square mounds are particularly concentrated in Izumo, with early examples of {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} also present. While generally small, square mounds were adopted for the tombs of powerful figures in the 7th century, when the construction of zenpō-kōen-fun ceased.
Examples include the {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Yōmei), measuring 63m x 60m, and the {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (designated as the tomb of Emperor Suiko), measuring 63m x 56m. The {{ill|Ishibutai Kofun|en|Ishibutai Kofun|WD=Q3816159|lt=Ishibutai Kofun}}, thought to be the tomb of {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}}, and the {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba Prefecture, with a side length of 80m and a height of 13m, are also noteworthy. The [[:en:Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun|Akasaka Tennōzan Kofun]] in Sakurai, measuring 45.5m x 42.2m with a height of 9.1m, has been linked to Emperor Sushun by scholar {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}.
The largest square mound in terms of size is {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, followed by Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun in Chiba Prefecture.
== Regional Distribution == === Tōhoku Region === *{{ill|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|en|Iinozaka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11666712|lt=Iinozaka Kofun Cluster}} (Natori, Miyagi Prefecture): Includes two square mounds (観音堂北一号墳 and 二号墳).
=== Kantō Region === * The {{ill|Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryūkakuji Iwaya Kofun|lt=Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|ja|龍角寺岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryukakuji Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} in Chiba is the largest square mound of the terminal Kofun period.
=== Chūbu and Tōkai Regions === * The {{ill|Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja|河合古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu includes the 30m-square mound {{ill|Jirōbei-zuka|ja|次郎兵衛塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jirōbei-zuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
=== Chūgoku Region === * The {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja|神原神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamihara Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神原神社}} in Shimane, an early square mound, measures 30m per side.
=== Kyūshū Region === * {{ill|Gionyama Kofun|ja|祇園山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gionyama Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuoka is 23m per side, dating to the mid-3rd century, and is hypothesized as {{ill|Himiko|en|Himiko|WD=Q234451|lt=Himiko}}'s tomb.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ishiyama Isao et al.|year=1979|title=Report on Gionyama Kofun Survey}}</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} * {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
